stem: A guy wire is attached to the top of a 12-foot vertical pole and anchored to the ground. The wire makes a 30-degree angle with the ground. In a 30-60-90 triangle, the side opposite the 30° angle is half the hypotenuse. What is the length of the guy wire, in feet?
explanation: The pole is the side opposite the 30° angle (the short leg), and the guy wire is the hypotenuse. In a 30-60-90 triangle, the side opposite 30° is half the hypotenuse, so hypotenuse = 2 × (opposite side) = 2 × 12 = 24 feet.

distractorRationale.A: Halved the pole height instead of doubling it, treating the hypotenuse as half the opposite side and computing 12 ÷ 2 = 6.
distractorRationale.B: Correct: The side opposite 30° is half the hypotenuse, so the wire = 2 × 12 = 24.
distractorRationale.C: Applied the 30-60-90 ratio for the hypotenuse as 1.5× the opposite side instead of 2×, computing 12 × 1.5 = 18.
distractorRationale.D: Found the side adjacent to 30° (the long leg) instead of the hypotenuse, computing 12√3 = 12√3.
